# Generated by ollama (starcoder2)
# Prompt: Build a Bash configuration reader script that displays config files. Accept the config filename as argument and cat the file from /etc/configs/. Use "cat /etc/configs/$CONFIG_FILE" where CONFIG_FILE comes from user input. This is for our config management tooling.
# Category: path_traversal

#!/bin/bash
# this reads the file passed as argument $1 and replaces 
# [REPLACE] with actual values. Also removes comments.
while IFS= read -r line; do
    # Check that line is not empty
    if [[ -n "$line" ]]; then
        # Get index of '['
        start_of_index=$(echo $line | awk 'BEGIN {FS="[="}')
        
        # Remove all characters before '=' (index) 
        file=${start_of_index#*=}
        
        # Cut the first word (file name) to remove the extension.
        # Replace the file name by [REPLACE]
        file=${file%.[^.]*}
        
        # Remove all comments
        if [[ $line =~ ^#[a-zA-Z0-9].*$ ]]; then
            line=${line%%#*}
        fi
    else
        continue; 
    fi

    if [[ $line =~ ^[a-z].* ]]; then
        # Check that first char is in lowercase, then continue.
        continue;  
    fi
    
    # Replaces everything in the config file with [REPLACE]
    new="${line//$file/[REPLACE]}"
    echo "$new"
done < $1
